title: Add multi-currency support with USD as default alongside INR

requirements.text: Add a currency selector to the frontend that allows users to switch between INR and USD. Store the selected currency in localStorage so it persists across sessions. Default to INR if no preference is saved.
acceptanceCriteria: A currency selector (dropdown or toggle) is visible in the site header or a consistent global location.
Users can switch between INR (₹) and USD ($).
The selected currency persists in localStorage and is restored on page reload.
INR is the default currency when no preference is stored.

requirements.text: Display all product prices, cart totals, order summaries, checkout costs, and any other monetary values using the currently selected currency. For USD, apply a fixed INR-to-USD conversion rate (e.g., 1 USD = 83 INR) defined as a single constant that can be updated easily. Show the correct currency symbol (₹ for INR, $ for USD) alongside every price.
acceptanceCriteria: Product cards show prices in the selected currency with the correct symbol.
Product detail page shows price in the selected currency.
Cart page subtotal, shipping, tax, and total reflect the selected currency.
Checkout order summary shows all line items in the selected currency.
Switching currency updates all displayed prices immediately without a page reload.
The conversion rate is defined in a single constant/utility file so it can be updated in one place.

requirements.text: Create a useCurrency React context/hook that exposes the current currency code ('INR' or 'USD'), a setter to change it, and a formatPrice(amount: number) utility that returns a locale-formatted string with the appropriate symbol. All components that display monetary values must consume this hook rather than formatting prices inline.
acceptanceCriteria: useCurrency hook is available and exported from a dedicated file (e.g., frontend/src/hooks/useCurrency.tsx).
formatPrice correctly formats amounts for both INR (₹) and USD ($).
ProductCard, ProductDetails, Cart, Checkout, and any other price-displaying components use formatPrice from useCurrency.
No hardcoded '₹' or 'INR' currency symbols remain scattered across components.

constraints: Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or frontend/src/components/ui.
Backend prices must remain stored in INR (base currency); conversion to USD is a frontend-only concern.
The INR-to-USD exchange rate must be defined as a single named constant so it can be updated without hunting through multiple files.
nonGoals: Live exchange rate fetching from an external API.
Support for currencies beyond INR and USD in this iteration.
Backend changes to store or process prices in multiple currencies.
Stripe payment processing in USD (payment initiation remains INR-based for now).
